query: Who is assigned to the task about implementing responsive navigation?
answer: Bob Martinez is assigned to task WRD-5, "Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer" [task:152].

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer
🧾 Summary Replace the existing nav with a responsive component that uses a hamburger drawer below 768px and a full horizontal layout above. Keyboard focus order and screen-reader landmark must be correct. 🪜 Motivation Current nav is two separate components — one rendered on desktop, one on mobile — that drift from each other constantly. Six bugs this quarter have been mobile-only or desktop-only nav regressions. Folding them into one responsive component eliminates that drift. ✅ Acceptance criteria Single Nav component renders correctly at 320px, 768px, 1024px, and 1440px breakpoints. Drawer traps focus when open and restores focus to the toggle on close. Escape closes the drawer. Skip-to-content link is the first focusable element.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Initial PR is up — drawer animation feels off below 320px (very small screens). Will tune the spring config.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Add a Storybook story for the 320px case so we don't regress it. Cypress can also pick it up if we run viewport tests.
</workspace_content>

matches.title: Spike: framer-motion vs native CSS animations for the redesign
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Leaning toward CSS-only for marketing. The drawer is the only animation that's actually tricky in pure CSS, and Bob already has a Web Animations API prototype that handles it.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Leaning toward CSS-only for marketing. The drawer is the only animation that's actually tricky in pure CSS, and Bob already has a Web Animations API prototype that handles it.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Confirmed — my prototype was 800 bytes vs framer-motion's 40KB. For marketing pages, no contest.
</workspace_content>
